title: Intermediaries, Hop-by-Hop Fields, Forwarding, and Transformation · Execution Trace: Hop By Hop Removal
summary: A proxy forwards Connection-nominated fields, copies raw forwarding claims, changes content without metadata repair, and assumes downstream and upstream share one connection and parser. Planned artifact: Add and verify the hop by hop removal increment in intermediary forwarding and transformation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.
Test client and server state transitions under delayed, missing, duplicate, fragmented, and rejected expectations without deadlock.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Hop By Hop Removal · Theory Hop By Hop Removal
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1228 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.
